firstPeoplesPrinciples.name: Connection to Country
firstPeoplesPrinciples.connection: Nature Walk: Spot the Living - Students explore the schoolyard, connecting to the land and observing living things in their local environment, honoring Traditional Owners' Country.
firstPeoplesPrinciples.activity: Aboriginal Plant Uses Walk: Identify plants that Traditional Peoples use for food, medicine, and tools. Students learn the Indigenous names where possible and understand that Country sustains all living things.

firstPeoplesPrinciples.name: Everything is Connected
firstPeoplesPrinciples.connection: Seed Sorting Adventure - Seeds are part of the生命周期 that connects plants, animals, and people together in Indigenous knowledge systems.
firstPeoplesPrinciples.activity: Story of the Seed: Share a story from Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander culture about how seeds came to be, teaching that all living things are related and care for each other.

firstPeoplesPrinciples.name: Country/Place
firstPeoplesPrinciples.connection: Growing Beans Time-Lapse - Understanding how plants grow connects students to the land and seasonal patterns that Traditional Peoples have observed for thousands of years.
firstPeoplesPrinciples.activity: Seasonal Planting Circle: Discuss when Country tells us to plant different seeds. Create a seasonal wheel showing when Traditional foods are harvested on Country.

firstPeoplesPrinciples.name: Culture is Connected to Land
firstPeoplesPrinciples.connection: Animal Classification Game - Animals have special significance in Indigenous cultures, each with totemic connections and cultural responsibilities.
firstPeoplesPrinciples.activity: Animal Totem Cards: Students learn about animals important to local Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander peoples, understanding that people and animals share responsibilities to care for Country.

firstPeoplesPrinciples.name: Spiritual Relationship
firstPeoplesPrinciples.connection: Our Bodies: Living Machines - Our bodies are gifts from our ancestors and connected to past, present, and future generations.
firstPeoplesPrinciples.activity: Body Mapping: Students draw their body and include things they receive from Country (food, shelter, medicine plants). Discuss how our bodies are connected to our family's and community's wellbeing.

firstPeoplesPrinciples.name: Learning from Observation
firstPeoplesPrinciples.connection: Goldfish Observation Station - Quiet observation is a traditional way of learning used by Indigenous peoples for millennia.
firstPeoplesPrinciples.activity: Lake/Marine Life Stories: Share stories from Indigenous cultures about water creatures. Students practice the respectful, quiet observation that Traditional fishers and rangers use.

firstPeoplesPrinciples.name: Two-Way Learning
firstPeoplesPrinciples.connection: Living vs Non-Living Sort - Understanding that Indigenous knowledge and Western science both have value and can teach each other.
firstPeoplesPrinciples.activity: Elder Storytelling Circle: Invite an Elder or knowledge holder to share knowledge about living things on Country. Students share what they learned and compare with classroom learning.

firstPeoplesPrinciples.name: Culture Informs Science
firstPeoplesPrinciples.connection: Plant Parts Investigation - Indigenous peoples have detailed botanical knowledge passed down over 65,000+ years.
firstPeoplesPrinciples.activity: Bush Tucker Garden Investigation: Explore edible and medicinal plant parts from Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander food traditions. Students taste safe plants and learn which parts of plants are food.
